name: Dulce Base New Mexico
description: The Dulce Base is a purported secret underground facility beneath Archuleta Mesa near Dulce, New Mexico. The conspiracy theory claims it is a joint human-alien base involved in advanced technology research, genetic experimentation, and other clandestine activities. The theory originated in the late 1970s and early 1980s, gaining traction through UFO enthusiasts and paranormal researchers. Proponents cite alleged testimonies from individuals claiming to have worked at the base, alongside purported leaked documents and anomalous geological formations as evidence. They describe multiple levels housing various alien species and human scientists, conducting ethically questionable experiments. Mainstream science and government sources dismiss the Dulce Base theory as a hoax or misinterpretation of legitimate underground facilities. No credible physical evidence has ever been presented to support its existence. Skeptics point to the lack of verifiable sources, inconsistencies in witness testimonies, and the implausibility of such a massive, undetectable facility existing without detection. The area around Dulce has seen legitimate military and scientific activity, which could contribute to misinterpretations and fuel the conspiracy. The theory continues to circulate online and within certain subcultures, fueled by mistrust of government and fascination with UFOs and extraterrestrial life.
subtitle: Secret underground human-alien facility conspiracy

claims.claim: The Dulce Base is a real underground facility.
claims.evidence: Testimonies of alleged former employees, purported leaked documents, and geological anomalies.
claims.criticism: No verifiable evidence, inconsistencies in testimonies, lack of physical proof, and explanation by natural phenomena or misinterpretation of legitimate activity.

claims.claim: The base is a joint human-alien operation.
claims.evidence: Accounts of various alien species residing and working at the base, collaborating with human scientists.
claims.criticism: Lacks any credible source or confirmation, contradicts scientific understanding of extraterrestrial life and interstellar travel.

claims.claim: The base conducts unethical experiments, including genetic manipulation.
claims.evidence: Claims of genetic experimentation on humans and animals, creating hybrid species.
claims.criticism: No verifiable evidence, relies on unsubstantiated claims and lacks scientific plausibility given the constraints of genetic engineering at the time the claims originated.

keyFigures.name: Phil Schneider
keyFigures.role: Civil engineer who claimed to have worked at the Dulce Base.

keyFigures.name: Paul Bennewitz
keyFigures.role: Physicist who claimed to have intercepted alien communications and discovered the base.

keyFigures.name: William Moore
keyFigures.role: Ufologist who initially promoted the Dulce Base story but later distanced himself from it.

keyFigures.name: Stanton Friedman
keyFigures.role: Ufologist known for debunking specific claims, including aspects of the Dulce Base narrative.

timeline.event: First reports and rumors of the Dulce Base begin to circulate.
Paul Bennewitz's claims gain widespread attention within UFO circles.
Phil Schneider's lectures and claims further popularize the Dulce Base conspiracy.
The Dulce Base theory persists online and in conspiracy theory subcultures.

sources.author: Curtis Howe
sources.work: An Examination of the Dulce Base Conspiracy
sources.title: Curtis Howe
